title: bupleurum petraeum var. incurvum
intro: Bupleurum petraeum var. incurvum, also known as Hare's ear or Rock Bupleurum, is a perennial plant in the carrot family, characterized by its distinctive, hairy, yellow-green flowers and deeply lobed leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Bupleurum petraeum var. incurvum include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
